reason: Primary acceptance criteria for OpenList/Alist scraping, poster wall, controlled classification settings, and no remote writes.
Architecture design that implementation must match for raw file scraping flow and classification settings.
Review token/credential redaction, local cache safety, and provider/AI data exposure boundaries.
Repository-level project expectations and raw file source scraping constraints.
Check raw file source scraping/classification contract, DataSource boundaries, root containment, and cache clearing behavior.
Check settings UI, controlled category editor, media/poster components, missing poster states, and route-level behavior.

Check TypeScript contracts, runtime validation, no broad any, and safe external/provider data mapping.
Check service/composable boundaries, external TMDB calls, Tauri IPC placement, and user-safe errors.

Check end-to-end data flow from provider listing through local cache to UI display.
Check reuse of existing DataSource/settings/media/persistence patterns and avoid duplicate constants/logic.
